title: coreopsis neaei
intro: Coreopsis neaei, also known as the Drummond's coreopsis or Tickseed, is a flowering plant species native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and slender stems. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce fever. The health benefits of Coreopsis neaei include its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation, making it a potential remedy for conditions such as arthritis and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis neaei can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
